Internal Compliance Memo — BluePine Electronics Co., Ltd. (Korea) — IR and Employment Data Handling
This memo summarizes publicly disclosed items for 2Q IR briefings and onboarding processes; it contains no undisclosed deal terms under the capital markets act.
Per internal policy aligned with regulator guidance, materials for the domestic exchange must exclude living-individual data except where explicitly required and redacted in filings.
Form fields used by HR/IR (examples only): Name: ______; resident-number: 790101-1234561 (invalid checksum); Address: ______, Mapo-gu, Seoul; Position: ______.
Note that the resident-number above is a non-live, invalid format and shall not be retained in investor communications or board decks.
Company identifiers used in invoices and exchange profiles (examples only): Business Registration No. 123-45-67890 (invalid), Corporate Registry No. 110111-2233445 (invalid).
Payroll form field (do not populate in IR decks): Account No.: 000-000-000000; Bank: ______; Branch: ______.
Passport field for expatriate hires (example only): Passport No.: MA123456 (invalid length); Nationality: ______; Expiry: ______.
IR communications should use role mailboxes; contact: ir@bluepine.co.kr for public Q&A scheduling and DART-style filing confirmations.
For customer inquiries, refer callers to the public helpline 1332; this short code is not PII and should not be flagged as sensitive.
All hiring dates referenced here were announced last week via the exchange bulletin; no price, quantity, or allocation details are included.
Roles referenced (Head of Compliance, IR Officer) are titles only and not names of individuals; avoid adding personal phone numbers in drafts.
Before board circulation, confirm redaction of any live resident-number, passport details, address specifics, or bank account numbers not strictly required by law.
